text: Delivered public Eigen.eigSymmetric (gale.spectral, shared, both platforms): dense path (full ascending solve via tred2/tql2 kernel; All/Count/IndexRange/ValueInterval realized as slices; lower-triangle only; residual diagnostics measured against the SYMMETRIZED matrix actually decomposed) and iterative operator path (Lanczos, CGS-twice full reorthogonalization over a GROWING subspace from a fixed deterministic LCG start vector — implementer replaced the assigned lossy restart after it collapsed clustered directions; growth is exact at m=n). Convergence semantics per parity doc: iterative partial/zero convergence -> Right(converged pairs + SpectralDiagnostics), requireConverged opt-in; structural violations -> typed Lefts incl. NonSquareMatrix for non-square operators, InvalidArgument k-bounds pointing at dense API, UnsupportedOperation for deferred shift-invert/Around (explicit target param as the deferral seam). Adversarially reviewed (fresh-context): no functional bugs; three review findings fixed by lead after implementer session stalled — (1) multiplicity-collapse caveat documented + characterizing test (single-vector Lanczos returns repeated eigenvalues once, e.g. diag(5,5,3,2,1) top-3 -> {2,3,5} with allConverged=true; inherent eigsh-class limitation), (2) overstated monotone-domination claim softened to Cauchy-interlacing extremes + exact-at-m=n, (3) dense residuals now measured against symmetrized matrix (garbled-upper-triangle test locks it). Also: clustered-spectrum growth test (1e-6 cluster, iterations>1), zero-convergence Right(empty) boundary test. Verified: coreJVM 217/217, coreJS 217/217, laws 17/17 both. Deferred for follow-up: shift-invert/Around wiring (LinearSolvePlan), implicit restarting as optimization.
